Fixed: `_maybe_inject_openai_computer_use` now takes `backend` and never injects on `backend="codex"`; `CodexOAuthAdapter` advertises `supports_computer_use=False` and its `computer_tool_param` returns `None` (no drift versus the now-excluded live path). The platform (PAYG) path is unchanged and still injects ‚Äî its backend ACCEPTANCE stays `unverified ‚Äî live test required` (no PAYG credentials available; CANNOT ¬ß4d). Guards: `tests/core/llm/adapters/test_openai_computer_use.py` (codex never injected, codex param `None`, platform unchanged).

## [0.99.223] - 2026-06-17

### Added
- **Computer-use Phase C ‚Äî OpenAI Responses GA `{type:"computer"}` wired end-to-end** (PR-COMPUTER-USE-C; operator-directed computer-use enhancement, the next v1.0.0 gate after Phase A; ctx7-grounded + Codex-MCP-reviewed). Phase A (v0.99.208) restored Anthropic computer-use on the live path; this extends it to OpenAI's GA computer tool on `gpt-5.5`, across BOTH live OpenAI adapters (`OpenAIPaygAdapter` platform + `CodexOAuthAdapter` codex). Full round-trip, not a half-wired offer: (1) request ‚Äî `_maybe_inject_openai_computer_use` injects the **bare** GA tool `{"type":"computer"}` into `build_responses_kwargs`, gated on `is_computer_use_enabled()` AND a GA-model frozenset (`gpt-5.5`; non-GA OpenAI models never get it), idempotent; (2) response ‚Äî `_normalize_computer_call` parses the distinct `computer_call` output item (batched GA `actions[]`, `call_id` correlation, `pending_safety_checks`) into a uniform `computer` tool_use; (3) replay ‚Äî a prior assistant `computer` tool_use re-emits as `computer_call` (not `function_call`) so it type-pairs with the output; (4) output ‚Äî `_maybe_computer_call_output` formats `computer_call_output` with a `computer_screenshot` data-URL (JPEG) + `acknowledged_safety_checks`; (5) handler ‚Äî `handle_computer` iterates the batched `actions[]`, remaps GA action shapes (`keypress` list‚Üícombo, `scroll_x/y`‚Üídirection, `drag` path‚Üísegment) to the harness, and ALWAYS returns a final screenshot (even on a final-action error) so the call is never left unanswered. Both adapters implement `ComputerUseCapable` (`supports_computer_use=True` + `computer_tool_param`). **Scope/attestation (CANNOT ¬ß4d):** ctx7 (`developers.openai.com` guides/tools-computer-use, 2026-06-17) confirms the GA request/response CONTRACT (bare tool, batched actions, screenshot output); the OpenAI/Codex backend's actual ACCEPTANCE of GEODE's request is marked `unverified ‚Äî live test required` in every relevant docstring and remains a live-test gate. Known host-harness gaps documented honestly (held-modifier `keys` and intermediate `drag` waypoints drop). 26 new guards (`tests/core/llm/adapters/test_openai_computer_use.py`, `tests/core/tools/test_computer_use.py`). Deferred to follow-ups per the plan: Phase E (sandbox), F (run_bash sandbox), D (Zhipu), B' (zoom).

## [0.99.222] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed
- **The `/model` picker list now reads routing constants live, closing the last boot-frozen site (H11-tail complete)** (PR-PRE10-H11-PICKER; pre-1.0 punch-list, the follow-up the v0.99.220 entry flagged). `core/cli/commands/_state.py` held `MODEL_PROFILES` as a module-level list (and `_MODEL_INDEX` derived from it), so the four routing-constant entries (`ANTHROPIC_SECONDARY` / `ANTHROPIC_BUDGET` / `OPENAI_PRIMARY` / `GLM_PRIMARY`) froze at import ‚Äî an operator editing `~/.geode/routing.toml` mid-session saw stale model ids/labels in `/model` until a restart, even though the runtime resolution path already re-read them (v0.99.220). Both are now factories ‚Äî `get_model_profiles()` / `get_model_index()` ‚Äî that do a function-local `from core.config import ‚Ä¶` per call. All ~14 consumers migrated in the same PR (no shim, per the 1-release-grace rule): `core/cli/commands/model.py` (caches the list once per function where used 2+ times), the `core.cli.commands` package re-exports, and 8 test modules. Guards: `tests/core/llm/test_h11_constant_reload.py::test_model_picker_list_reads_live` / `::test_model_picker_index_reads_live` (patch `core.config.*` ‚Üí picker reflects the new value). With this, **0 boot-frozen routing-constant sites remain**.

## [0.99.221] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed
- **HITL result-feedback tools now persist the operator's verdict instead of dropping it** (PR-PRE10-ROUND2; pre-1.0 punch-list #4, Codex-MCP-reviewed). `rate_result` / `accept_result` / `reject_result` (`core/cli/tool_handlers/hitl.py`) wrote the verdict to a closure-local dict (`_human_ratings` / `_result_feedback`) that nothing ever read. They now fire a new `HookEvent.RESULT_FEEDBACK`, which the wildcard RunLog subscriber persists to the session JSONL (surfaced by `geode history`). New `core/hooks/tool_hooks.py` (`set_tool_hooks` / `fire_tool_hook`) holds the shared HookSystem injection in `core.hooks` so cross-layer tool handlers ‚Äî including `core.cli` ‚Äî can fire without bootstrap importing the CLI layer (the Server-never-CLI import contract; a first cut that put the setter in `hitl.py` broke it, caught by `lint-imports`). `GeodeBootstrap` gained a `hook_system` field so `propagate_to_thread()` re-injects the ctx into daemon/IPC worker threads (feedback persists in serve mode, not just REPL). Full ActivityRow typed-coverage cascade: new `ResultFeedbackRow` + `ResultFeedbackDetails` + `_TypedRowSpec` + `TypedActivityRow` union member + `__all__`; HookEvent count 62‚Üí63 across tests + docs (GEODE.md, hook-system.md/.en.md, activity*.py). Guard: `tests/core/cli/test_hitl_feedback_persist.py`.

### GAP Audit (punch-list #4)
- Dropped (already resolved / mis-flagged): the sync `ApprovalWorkflow` API was already removed; `prospect` / `full_pipeline` are **live** plan templates (`core/orchestration/plan_mode.py`), not stale enum values. Only the result-feedback dead dict was a genuine remnant, now wired.

## [0.99.220] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed
- **Routing constants in the model-resolution call path are now read live, not boot-frozen (H11-tail)** (PR-PRE10-H11-TAIL; pre-1.0 punch-list, Codex-MCP-reviewed). `reload_routing_constants()` rebinds `core.config.{ANTHROPIC,OPENAI,CODEX,GLM}_*` in place on a `routing.toml` reload, and function-local `from core.config import X` reads re-resolve the live value ‚Äî but several **module-level by-value** importers held boot-time copies, so an operator editing `~/.geode/routing.toml` mid-session got stale models until a restart. De-frozen across the call path: provider failover chains (`core/llm/providers/{anthropic,openai,glm}.py` + `provider_dispatch.py` lambdas now match the codex entry's live `__import__` pattern), the OpenAI default model (`OpenAIAdapter.__init__` resolves `OPENAI_PRIMARY` lazily), the streaming retry chain (`router/calls/streaming.py`), the AgenticLoop's no-`act_model` Anthropic fallback (`agent_loop.py`), and the sub-agent default model (`core/skills/agents.py` `AgentDefinition.model` uses a `default_factory`; the 3 built-in `_DEFAULT_AGENTS` specs drop their frozen `"model"` key so the factory fills it live). Dead `DEFAULT_*_MODEL` / `*_FALLBACK_MODELS` provider aliases (glm/codex/openai/anthropic, no consumers) were removed. Guards: `tests/core/llm/test_h11_constant_reload.py` (patch `core.config.*` ‚Üí consumers see the new value); `test_failover.py` repointed to the live `core.config.ANTHROPIC_FALLBACK_CHAIN` SoT. **Scope note (per Codex review):** this de-freezes the *runtime model-resolution path*; one frozen site remains ‚Äî `core/cli/commands/_state.py` `MODEL_PROFILES` (the `/model` picker label/id list, ~12 CLI consumers) ‚Äî tracked as a separate follow-up (display + picker-id only, not the core resolution path).

## [0.99.219] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed
- **Placeholder env values no longer seed junk auth profiles** (PR-PRE10-HYGIENE; pre-1.0 punch-list, follow-up to PR-READINESS-OAUTH). `build_auth` (`core/wiring/container.py`) and `migrate_env_to_toml` (`core/auth/auth_toml.py`) seeded an API-key `AuthProfile` for any non-empty `settings.*_api_key`, skipping only empty values ‚Äî so a stale `.env.example` placeholder (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...`) created a real-looking profile that polluted the rotator and slipped past readiness (the gap PR-READINESS-OAUTH had to defend at the readiness layer). Both seed sites now skip placeholders. The rule is centralized as `core.config.env_io.is_placeholder` ‚Äî previously a private `_is_placeholder` in `core/wiring/startup.py`, now the single SoT that readiness (`_has_any_llm_key` / `_has_available_profile`), `.env` auto-generation, and both seeders share (startup delegates via a lazy import that keeps its module pydantic-free). Guard: `tests/core/auth/test_auth_toml.py::test_migration_skips_placeholder_keys`.

### Changed
- **Config deprecation warnings now name a concrete removal version** (same PR). The `[self_improving_loop.petri.*]` and `[self_improving_loop.mutator]` migration `DeprecationWarning`s said "removed in a future release"; they now commit to **v1.1.0** (the first minor after the v1.0.0 stable), giving operators a real deadline to move to `[self_improving_loop.autoresearch.<role>]`. A 1.0 contract needs a stated deprecation horizon.

## [0.99.218] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed
- **Two dead sub-agent guardrail knobs wired to actually enforce** (PR-CONFIG-SLOP-SWEEP; operator-directed config/design slop sweep, Codex-MCP-reviewed). `max_total_subagents` (default 15) and `subagent_max_tokens` (default 32768) were each declared on `Settings` AND mapped into the `config.toml` cascade, but no runtime code read them ‚Äî so an operator setting `[subagent] max_total` / `max_tokens` got silently ignored (the same "setting surface with no wired consumer" slop class as PR-HERMES-ENV-ALIGN's empty `.env` placeholders). Now `max_total_subagents` is enforced as a per-session cap in `SubAgentManager.adelegate` (a monotonic `_spawned_total` counter reserved under the records lock; overflow tasks get an explicit failed `SubResult` instead of silently spawning), and `subagent_max_tokens` is threaded via `WorkerRequest` into the spawned `AgenticLoop`'s `max_tokens` (was a hardcoded `32768`). `services.py` passes both from settings; the default `max_total` stays 15 (operator-approved ceiling).
- **`config.toml` values now actually reach the worker spawn** (same PR, Codex review catch). `SubAgentManager._build_worker_request` built a *fresh* `Settings()` (env + defaults only ‚Äî the config.toml overlay applies only to the reloaded singleton), so `[subagent] max_tokens` / `[model]` were silently dropped for every spawned worker; it now reads the singleton. Relatedly, `SharedServices` calls `reload_settings_from_disk()` **before** building the per-session `SubAgentManager` (was after), so the manager's caps (`max_subagent_depth` + `max_total_subagents`) initialize from the config.toml-applied singleton, not a stale pre-reload one.

### Changed
- **Worker `WorkerRequest.model` / `provider` defaults are now inherit-sentinels (`""`) instead of a frozen `claude-opus-4-6` / `anthropic` literal** (same PR). The production spawn always sets both explicitly; the empty sentinel keeps a directly-constructed request from pinning a now-stale model ‚Äî `_run_agentic` resolves `request.model or settings.model` (+ `_resolve_provider` for the matching provider), mirroring the sibling `time_budget_s = 0.0 (= inherit)` convention.
- **De-jargoned two user-facing help strings** (same PR): the `/model` reflection-role description ("Belief-update node (PR-3 C-2)" ‚Üí "Reviews and refines the agent's reasoning after each tool batch") and `/audit-seeds` ("Seed-pipeline candidate generation (co-scientist 7-role)" ‚Üí "Generate and score candidate evaluation seeds"). Internal design nomenclature was leaking into the model picker / `/help`.
- **`learning_extract_model` free-tier footgun made visible** (same PR): the default `glm-4.7-flash` rides a limited-time free tier; the field description + `model_pricing.toml` comment now warn that the call bills at the standard GLM rate when the promo lapses, so cost-watchers can re-pin a known-priced model.

### Removed
- **Two dead `Settings` fields deleted** (same PR): `agentic_loop_time_budget` (real wall-clock budgeting uses `time_budget_s`) and `checkpoint_db` (vestigial), each removed together with its `config.toml` mapping. New guard `tests/core/config/test_no_dead_settings_fields.py` fails CI if any non-secret `Settings` field has zero consumers outside config/tests, so a future dead knob can't ship. Noted follow-up: `build_auth` / `migrate_env_to_toml` still seed API-key profiles from placeholder env values (rotator/auth hygiene, separate PR).

## [0.99.217] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed
- **Readiness no longer forces a subscription-OAuth-only operator into dry-run** (PR-READINESS-OAUTH; follow-up to PR-HERMES-ENV-ALIGN, Codex-MCP-reviewed). `check_readiness` gated the `LLM Analysis` capability and `blocked` / `force_dry_run` on `_has_any_llm_key()` alone, so an operator who authenticates by subscription OAuth and keeps no raw key in `.env` was counted as having no credential and pushed into dry-run. Fix: `has_credential = has_key or oauth_provider is not None or _has_available_profile()` now gates the capability, `blocked`, and `force_dry_run`, covering all three credential origins the call path actually uses ‚Äî (a) a raw provider key, (b) a Codex-CLI OAuth login (`~/.codex/auth.json`, via `oauth_provider = detect_subscription_oauth() if not has_key else None`), and (c) any usable profile in the ProfileStore, chiefly a GEODE-owned `/login` credential hydrated from `~/.geode/auth.toml` (new `_has_available_profile()`, which mirrors the dispatch `is_available` eligibility filter so readiness and `_resolve_codex_token` agree on what counts as usable). The probes short-circuit (run only when the cheaper checks fail), so the raw-key path never builds the ProfileStore nor touches the OAuth probe. The Codex-CLI token is treated expiry-blind on both sides (Codex CLI refreshes its own token; `read_codex_cli_credentials` re-reads it), so readiness and dispatch stay consistent. `_has_available_profile()` applies the same placeholder rule as `_has_any_llm_key` (`p.key and not _is_placeholder(p.key)`) ‚Äî `build_auth` / `migrate_env_to_toml` seed an API-key profile from any non-empty env value without a placeholder check, so a stale `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...` would otherwise yield an `is_available` profile that passes readiness while the raw-key path rejects it. The GEODE-owned-profile origin and the placeholder-profile parity were both Codex MCP review catches ‚Äî the first cut fixed only the codex-CLI path. Guards: `test_oauth_only_not_dry_run`, `test_geode_login_profile_not_dry_run`, `test_placeholder_profile_does_not_unblock`, `test_no_credential_anywhere_is_dry_run`; the two no-credential tests patch the probes so they stay deterministic on a host with real `~/.codex` / `~/.geode/auth.toml` creds.

### Changed
- **`auto_generate_env` emits empty/placeholder secret keys COMMENTED, not as active blanks** (same PR). It copied `.env.example` lines like `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=` verbatim ‚Äî an empty value is not a placeholder per `_is_placeholder`, so an active blank `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=` landed in the generated project `.env`: the exact shadow shape PR-HERMES-ENV-ALIGN had to defend against, and a confusing "key present but blank" read. Now an empty or placeholder value is written as `# KEY=` (commented), so a generated `.env` documents the available keys without an active entry that could shadow the global `~/.geode/.env`. `.env.example` itself now comments its secret keys and the `GEODE_MODEL` line (an active project-level model pin would override the global default chain) and documents the subscription-login path. Guard: `tests/core/wiring/test_startup.py::TestAutoGenerateEnv` updated to assert the commented form.

## [0.99.216] - 2026-06-15

### Fixed
- **Secret `.env` precedence flipped to global-authoritative (Hermes-aligned) ‚Äî a project `.env` no longer shadows global API keys** (PR-HERMES-ENV-ALIGN; operator-directed, benchmarked against the ~/workspace agent harnesses). Symptom: `geode` reported "No LLM API key set" / dry-run despite real keys living in `~/.geode/.env`. Root cause chain: C-3 (v0.99.167) made the project `.env` beat the global `~/.geode/.env` (pydantic `env_file=(global, ".env")`, later-file-wins), and `welcome.auto_generate_env` writes a project `.env` from `.env.example` with **empty** key placeholders (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=`) ‚Äî so the empty project value shadowed the real global key (pydantic, unlike `env_io.load_env_files`, does not skip empty values). A survey of codex / claude-code / openclaw / hermes / crumb / paperclip found all six keep secrets global-authoritative or empty-never-shadows; GEODE was the only one auto-generating empty placeholders under project-wins. Fix (Hermes pattern): flip BOTH loaders ‚Äî pydantic `env_file=(".env", global)` and `env_io.load_env_files` order `(.env, global)` ‚Äî so the **global `~/.geode/.env` is the authoritative secret store and a project `.env` only fills keys it lacks, never shadows a global key**. `config.toml` keeps project>global (config is per-project tunable; a credential has one global source). Both loaders flip together so the C-3 H5 per-process inversion stays fixed. `config explain` coalesces an empty `.env` value to None so it never registers as a winning layer. Guards: `tests/core/config/test_c3_env_loading.py` (global-beats-project + empty-project-doesn't-shadow-global, both the os.environ promotion and the pydantic paths) + `test_config_explain.py`.

## [0.99.215] - 2026-06-14

### Architecture
- **Self-improving state split by lifecycle: tracked SoT in-repo, runtime out-of-repo** (PR-STATE-SOT-RUNTIME-SPLIT; operator-directed). The CSP-7 repo-root `state/` (which mixed both) splits into two homes: the git-TRACKED SoT ‚Äî `mutations.jsonl`, `baseline_archive.jsonl`, `baseline_epochs.json`, `policies/`, `seed_pools/`, `results.{tsv,jsonl}` ‚Äî moves in-repo to `core/self_improving/state/` (data-in-package, colocated with `core.paths.AUTORESEARCH_STATE_DIR`); the RUNTIME scratch ‚Äî `baseline.json`, `run.log`, `handoff/`, `campaign/`, per-run `seed_generation/` ‚Äî moves out-of-repo to `~/.geode/self-improving/` (`core.paths.RUNTIME_ROOT`) so worktree/clone runs no longer scatter runtime files into git nor lose them on `git clean`. `seed_pools/` is always repo-pinned (operator decision D-3).

### Fixed
- **Campaign worker isolation restored under the split** ‚Äî when `GEODE_STATE_ROOT` is set (path-independent worker subprocesses), BOTH the tracked SoT and the runtime root collapse to `$GEODE_STATE_ROOT/autoresearch/` (matching `campaign._seed_isolated_state_root`), so concurrent workers never race on / pollute the in-repo `mutations.jsonl` and read `baseline.json` from their own seeded tree. Pinned by `tests/core/test_paths_env_override.py`.
- **`baseline.json` readers no longer derive it as a sibling of the tracked `mutations.jsonl`** ‚Äî the MCP `self_improving_status` payload, `/self-improving status` + `rollback-check`, `rubric_excerpts` injection, and `outer-bundle` now read the runtime `core.paths.BASELINE_JSON_PATH` directly (the split moved them to different roots).
- **Hub builder (`build_self_improving_hub.py`) reads each datum from its lifecycle home** ‚Äî tracked ledgers/policies from `--state-dir`, the runtime baseline/campaign-progress/gen-0-snapshot from `RUNTIME_ROOT`, and the in-repo audit MANIFEST from the new `--repo-root`. `restore_sot`, campaign snapshot/checkpoint dirs, `train.py` fallback, and `extract_baseline_transcripts` likewise corrected to lifecycle constants.

## [0.99.214] - 2026-06-14

### Changed
- **All 60 tool descriptions audited against the Fable 5 standard; 35 rewritten** (PR-TOOL-DESC-FABLE5; operator-directed, continuing the Fable 5 contrast). Triage of `core/tools/definitions.json` found mostly "use when user X" one-liners with almost no when-NOT-to-use (4/60), almost no examples (5/60), unresolved sibling-trigger collisions, and stale Game-IP/pipeline vocabulary. Rewrote 35 to add sibling-disambiguation and purge dead vocabulary: the **memory_save ‚Üî note_save** collision (both fired on "remember this / save this") now cross-references (GEODE-derived insight vs user-dictated note); memory_search/note_read/session_search, create_plan/task_create, and schedule_job/trigger_event/calendar_create_event likewise disambiguated; "analysis result/plan" ‚Üí "result"/"execution plan", and the Game-IP `genre` example dropped from generate_data. The 24 already-Fable-5-quality descriptions (run_bash, web_fetch, llms_txt_index, petri_audit, the file tools, arxiv/seed tools) were left as-is. Guard: `tests/core/tools/test_tool_descriptions_fable5.py`.

### Removed
- **Vestigial `rerun_node` tool deleted** (same PR). Its handler hardcoded `allowed: set[str] = set()`, so every call returned "Cannot rerun ‚Ä¶ Allowed: []" ‚Äî the success path was unreachable ‚Äî and it referenced pipeline "nodes" removed in the LangGraph purge (v0.99.149/206). Removed the tool definition, `handle_rerun_node` + its handler-map entry (`core/cli/tool_handlers/hitl.py`), the `reject_result` hint that pointed at it, and the docstring mentions; tool count 60 ‚Üí 59 (`GEODE.md` Identity + Architecture synced). The sibling `rate_result`/`accept_result`/`reject_result` handlers are kept but flagged ‚Äî they record feedback into closure-local dicts with no downstream reader (a separate dead-code follow-up: wire or remove).

## [0.99.213] - 2026-06-14

### Added
- **Three Fable 5-parity policies added to the always-on runtime prompt** (PR-PROMPT-POLICY-ADDITIONS; operator-directed, continuing the Fable 5 contrast). (1) **Unrecognized-entity search discipline** + (2) **even-handedness on contested topics** as a new `## Answering discipline` block in `router.md`'s `<system>`: do not answer about a product/release/person/work you cannot place ‚Äî search first (knowing a franchise ‚âÝ knowing its newest release); and present contested political/ethical topics as each side's strongest case rather than asserting one view. (3) **Source fidelity & copyright** section in the agentic suffix: paraphrase fetched material by default, no long verbatim passages from a single source, never reproduce lyrics/poems/whole articles, and a report is your own synthesis ‚Äî clarifying that Grounding rule 4's "quote the exact number" covers data points, not prose (the one line that could read as license to quote at length). All three live in the always-on baseline (survive `GEODE_PERSONA=off` / audit-mode). Prompt hash pins re-stamped (ROUTER_SYSTEM / AGENTIC_SUFFIX). Guard: `tests/integration/test_prompt_audit_2026_05_12.py::test_fable5_followup_policies_present`.

## [0.99.212] - 2026-06-14

### Changed
- **GEODE persona/soul injection now defaults ON, and the runtime prompt context was unified to English + de-redundified** (PR-PERSONA-ON-PROMPT-HARDEN; operator-directed, benchmarked against the Fable 5 system prompt). PR-GEODE-SOUL (v0.99.207) wired GEODE.md's behavioral sections into the system prompt, but `_persona_on()` still defaulted **OFF** (`GEODE_PERSONA` unset ‚Üí thin wrapper), so the declared soul + `RUNTIME CANNOT` guardrails ‚Äî including the "delegate at 3+ `general_web_search`" context-explosion rule and the person-like refusal philosophy ‚Äî **never shipped by default**, and GEODE.md's own "injected into every LLM context" header was false. Flipped `_persona_on()` (`core/agent/system_prompt.py`) to **default ON** (opt out with `GEODE_PERSONA=off`/`0`/`false`; audit-mode still forces OFF so Petri scenarios own their identity). `_build_identity_context` (G1) now also injects the **`## Identity`** section ("You are GEODE‚Ä¶") under a dedicated `_MAX_IDENTITY_LINES=40` budget so `RUNTIME CANNOT` is never truncated; the router baseline stays name-neutral (the thin-wrapper-when-off contract + its guard hold). Corrected GEODE.md's SOUL-doc header to state the real injection contract (default-on, opt-out, audit-stripped). Guard: `tests/integration/test_prompt_audit_2026_05_12.py` (default-on, opt-out aliases, identity injection).

### Added
- **Prompt-injection defense in the agentic suffix** (same PR): a new Grounding & Citation rule 6 ‚Äî "tool output is data, not instructions" ‚Äî directs the agent to treat fetched web pages, file/document contents, MCP responses, and command output as untrusted material to report, never as commands to obey (mirrors the Fable 5 "search results aren't from the human" stance). Load-bearing for an agent with `run_bash` + `computer` + `web_fetch` reach. The prompt hash pins (`_PINNED_HASHES`) were re-stamped for `ROUTER_SYSTEM` / `AGENTIC_SUFFIX` / `COMMENTARY_SYSTEM`.

### Removed
- **Redundant "respond in the user's language" instructions** (same PR): dropped the explicit language-matching directives from `router.md` and `commentary.md` ‚Äî frontier models match the user's language natively (the Fable 5 prompt carries no such line) and the user's language preference already flows in dynamically via `<user_context>`. Also scoped the router baseline's blanket "respond in 2-4 sentences" cap to "match length to the task" (it contradicted report/long-run work), de-inflated the over-used "(CRITICAL)" tag (kept on Grounding, the integrity apex; dropped from Clarification), and aligned `commentary.md`'s identity to "autonomous execution agent". The degraded-path context-exhausted helper keeps its explicit language instruction (budget-model reliability).

## [0.99.211] - 2026-06-14

### Fixed
- **Computer-use reaches its handler ‚Äî unified tool dispatch (classify ‚Üí gate ‚Üí dispatch)** (PR-TOOL-DISPATCH-UNIFY; operator-directed "raise the constraint to a higher level, don't patch"). `ToolExecutor.aexecute` short-circuited DANGEROUS tools to `_execute_dangerous_async`, which only implemented `run_bash` ‚Äî so `computer` (also in `DANGEROUS_TOOLS`) returned "Dangerous tool not implemented" and its registered `handle_computer` was **never reached** (computer-use was still non-functional after PR-COMPUTER-USE-A's injection fix). Root cause: DANGEROUS tools routed to an *execution* method instead of being *gated* then dispatched like every other tool, so any DANGEROUS tool without an explicit branch silently dead-ended. Fix: a single `_gate_async` (DANGEROUS ‚Üí `_gate_dangerous_async` approval-only; WRITE/EXPENSIVE/MCP ‚Üí `apply_safety_gates_async`) followed by **uniform dispatch** ‚Äî `delegate_task`, `run_bash` (`_run_bash_exec_async`, validation+approval moved to the gate), `computer`, and every registered handler now flow through the same path, so a registered handler is always reachable and a DANGEROUS tool with no explicit gate branch FAILS CLOSED (refuses to dispatch unapproved, even with a handler ‚Äî Codex review) instead of a bespoke dead-end. `computer` is gated by `ApprovalWorkflow.confirm_computer_async` (session-level approval ‚Äî continuous control makes per-action HITL impractical; auto under `--dangerously-skip-permissions` / open HITL). Removed the now-dead `_execute_dangerous_async` and the unused `_confirm_write` / `_confirm_cost` executor wrappers. Guard: `tests/core/agent/test_tool_dispatch_unify.py`. (The dead-in-production sync `ApprovalWorkflow` API ‚Äî `apply_safety_gates`/`confirm_*`/`request_bash_approval` sync twins ‚Äî is a separable legacy-cleanup follow-up; its removal requires migrating the B6 parallel-approval incident tests to async.)

## [0.99.210] - 2026-06-14

### Infrastructure
- **Removed the `auto-backmerge.yml` workflow; back-merge is now a deliberate pre-sync step** (operator-directed ‚Äî "dev‚Üîmain back-merges are too many"). The workflow fired on every push to `main` and opened a `main ‚Üí develop` PR whenever develop lagged; because the feature/main divergence is never a fast-forward, those PRs never auto-merged and piled up (10 open at once). The only recurring drift is the kanban (`docs/progress.md`, committed on main post-merge). Replaced by a mandatory **`main ‚Üí develop` sync before every `develop ‚Üí main` merge** (documented in CLAUDE.md ¬ß6) ‚Äî one deliberate back-merge per cycle that pulls the prior kanban into develop, so develop never merges to main while lagging and the drift self-heals instead of accumulating. Also switched **`feature ‚Üí develop` to squash-merge** (one commit per change, collapsing the per-PR Codex-round fix commits and the per-feature merge commit) to further cut main-history noise; `develop ‚Üí main` stays `--merge`. Cleared the 10 stale auto-backmerge PRs (#2265‚Äì#2283, kanban-only).

## [0.99.209] - 2026-06-14

### Added
- **`--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag** (Claude Code parity; operator-directed). A single opt-in for a frictionless autonomous agent experience: when set, EVERY HITL gate is bypassed (write/expensive/bash/MCP approval, incl. the parallel-tool batch-cost prompt) AND the plan-approval stop is skipped ‚Äî after `create_plan` the agent runs the plan immediately instead of waiting for `approve_plan` ("treat the plan stop like any other HITL gate"). Wiring: `core/config/_settings.py` `dangerously_skip_permissions` (env `GEODE_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS`); the `geode --dangerously-skip-permissions` callback (`core/cli/__init__.py`) sets the env (so a freshly auto-started daemon inherits it) + prints a warning banner; the thin CLI advertises it in the `client_capability` handshake (`core/cli/ipc_client.py`), and the daemon adopts it per connection (`core/server/ipc_server/poller.py::_adopt_skip_permissions`). **The flag is PER-SESSION state, not a process-global**: `_adopt_skip_permissions` sets a `ContextVar` (`core/agent/safety.py::set_skip_permissions`) in the connection's task/thread before its first prompt, and the HITL gates (`ApprovalWorkflow`) + the plan handler read it at call time via `current_skip_permissions()` (which falls back to the `settings` env default when unset, so a daemon launched in skip mode applies daemon-wide). Per-session isolation is the load-bearing property: a construction-time read missed a running daemon and stuck ON for the next client (Codex BLOCKER), and a process-global read let a skip client flip approvals for ANOTHER session concurrently awaiting tool work (Codex HIGH) ‚Äî the ContextVar avoids both. Bypass covers the write/expensive/bash/MCP gates **and** the parallel-tool `batch_cost_approval` (Codex HIGH). The plan handler (`core/cli/tool_handlers/plan.py`) treats it as `plan_auto_execute`. The effective flag is forwarded into sub-agent worker subprocesses (`core/orchestration/isolated_execution.py` ‚Äî whitelisted + set from `current_skip_permissions()` at spawn) so a write-capable sub-agent under the flag also bypasses HITL in its worker (no IPC handshake there ‚Üí reads the env default); thread-mode sub-agents inherit the ContextVar directly (Codex HIGH). The headless tool-deny filter (`_HEADLESS_DENIED_TOOLS` for Slack/cron) is intentionally left intact ‚Äî a separate unattended-channel safety concern. Guards: `tests/core/server/test_dangerously_skip_permissions.py` (env fallback, ContextVar override, per-session isolation, gate bypass for write/bash/mcp/batch, adopt, capability advertise) + plan-handler auto-execute tests.

## [0.99.208] - 2026-06-14

### Fixed
- **Computer-use resurrected on the production agentic path** (PR-COMPUTER-USE-A, Phase A of the computer-use enhancement; operator-directed). Two latent defects meant the `computer` tool never actually worked in production: (1) the `computer_20251124` tool was injected ONLY in the legacy `ClaudeAgenticAdapter.agentic_call` request builder, but PR-MAINPATH-67 (2026-05-24) deleted that dispatch branch ‚Äî the production AgenticLoop reaches Anthropic through `LLMAdapter.acomplete` ‚Üí `anthropic_oauth`/`anthropic_payg` ‚Üí `_anthropic_common.build_create_kwargs`/`build_stream_kwargs`, which never injected the tool, so the model was never offered `computer` at all (the same docstring-vs-live-path class of bug the tool-search-defer review caught); (2) the single-tool handler (`core/cli/tool_handlers/single_tool.py::handle_computer`) read `action` with `.get` and then passed it BOTH positionally and inside `**kwargs` to `aexecute`, raising "got multiple values for argument 'action'" on every non-default call (latent because the tool was never live-exercised). Fix: a shared `_anthropic_common._maybe_inject_computer_use` injects the tool + the **model-aware** beta header (merge, never clobber an existing `anthropic-beta`) on both live builders when `is_computer_use_enabled()` ‚Äî `computer_20251124` + `computer-use-2025-11-24` for the current generation (Opus 4.8/4.7/4.6, Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.5, and future models), `computer_20250124` + `computer-use-2025-01-24` for the legacy set (Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5; `ANTHROPIC_COMPUTER_USE_LEGACY_MODELS` in `model_capabilities.py`) ‚Äî verified against the Anthropic computer-use docs (CANNOT ¬ß4d; the SDK Literal lags, so the newer string is sent as a plain `str`). The dated GEODE id suffix (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`) is stripped before the legacy lookup so dated legacy ids resolve correctly. It injects even with no registry tools, dedups by the native tool type (a custom same-name tool can't suppress it), and the type-carrying tool is exempt from tool-search defer. Display dims come from the harness `TARGET_WIDTH`/`TARGET_HEIGHT` (single SoT). The handler now `pop`s `action`. (3) **The screenshot now returns as an Anthropic `tool_result` IMAGE block** (`ToolCallProcessor._serialize_computer_result`), not base64 text ‚Äî previously the model could not *see* the screen on the next turn AND the token guard / offload store truncated/offloaded the huge base64 string; the image path bypasses both. The non-image fields stay as a compact text block. (Codex review: HIGH "model is blind", LOW custom-name dedup.)

### Added
- **`ComputerUseCapable` adapter protocol** (same PR): `core/llm/adapters/base.py`, mirroring `WebSearchCapable` at the source-adapter level so computer-use support is enumerable. `AnthropicOAuthAdapter` / `AnthropicPaygAdapter` declare `supports_computer_use=True` + `computer_tool_param()`, which returns the exact param the live builder injects (drift pinned by test). This is the contract Phase C (OpenAI `{type:"computer"}`) and Phase D (Zhipu) extend. Guards: `tests/core/llm/adapters/test_computer_use_live_path.py` + the handler regression in `tests/core/tools/test_computer_use.py`. Plan + ctx7 grounding: `docs/plans/2026-06-14-computer-use-enhancement.md`.

## [0.99.207] - 2026-06-14

### Changed
- **GEODE.md SOUL doc rebalanced toward behavioral identity + the behavioral half wired into the system prompt** (PR-GEODE-SOUL; operator-directed, benchmarked against the Fable 5 system prompt + frontier agent-identity docs). GEODE.md is the Tier-0 SOUL injected into every LLM context, but it was ~85% architecture reference and ~15% behavior ‚Äî it had no voice/tone, anti-sycophancy, refusal-tone, persistence, or output-norm guidance, the half every frontier system prompt is mostly made of. Added (second person): a **Voice & Conduct** section (direct/concise, warm-without-flattery, honest-over-agreeable, no-slop), **Operating Principles** (persistence "keep going until resolved, don't guess", progress-narration, plus the prior evidence/bias/second-opinion/degrade principles), a refusal-philosophy line in **RUNTIME CANNOT** (decline like a person, proportionate, offer an alternative), a tool-use philosophy + a by-example "How you act" block in **Tool Use**. Compressed the Architecture section (was 35% of the file, deep wiring constants) to a capability summary, keeping the honest isolation-boundary contract and the grounded model table; moved the "English in LLM-context files" authoring rule to CLAUDE.md. **Wiring (the load-bearing part)**: `core/agent/system_prompt.py::_build_identity_context` (G1) parses GEODE.md by EXACT section header ‚Äî it injected only `Core Principles + RUNTIME CANNOT + Defaults`. Repointed it to the BEHAVIORAL sections (`Voice & Conduct + Operating Principles + RUNTIME CANNOT`) and dropped the numeric `Defaults` (reference, not identity) from the system prompt, so the new conduct guidance actually reaches the model; also strip cross-reference blockquotes from the injection. Guard: `tests/core/agent/test_geode_soul_g1_behavioral.py` pins that the behavioral sections reach G1 (a future header rename can't silently disconnect the SOUL again).

### Fixed
- **GEODE.md internal numeric contradictions corrected against code** (same PR): tool count `59` ‚Üí **60** (`core/tools/definitions.json`), hook events `81` ‚Üí **62** (the file also said 62 elsewhere; `HookEvent` enum = 62), Lane global concurrency `8` ‚Üí **50** (`DEFAULT_GLOBAL_CONCURRENCY`), SubAgent timeout `120s` ‚Üí **600s** (`core/agent/worker.py` / `sub_agent.py` default, clamped 10..3600) ‚Äî a SOUL doc injected into every context must not carry wrong constants ("measured values only"). Removed the stale LangGraph-era `Node contract` / `Reducer fields (Annotated[list, operator.add])` conventions ‚Äî the core runs the AgenticLoop, not a graph (cf. PR-LANGGRAPH-PURGE). Also corrected the safety-contract descriptions to match code (Codex review, 3 rounds): `DANGEROUS_TOOLS` = `run_bash` + `computer` (not `delegate_task`, which the old table wrongly listed); the permission tiers now state that approval is decided at runtime by the approval gate from `hitl_level` + standing Always-grants + read-only detection (mode-dependent, not a fixed threshold), rather than overclaiming "always requires HITL"; IPC = hitl=2 (no headless deny filter); headless DAEMON/SCHEDULER deny `run_bash` + `delegate_task` (`_HEADLESS_DENIED_TOOLS`, a separate list from DANGEROUS_TOOLS). A SOUL injected into every context must not assert a stronger safety contract than the runtime enforces.

## [0.99.206] - 2026-06-14

### Removed
- **Vestigial LangGraph dependency + dead graph-runtime surface purged** (PR-LANGGRAPH-PURGE; operator-directed). The in-tree analysis graph (the Game-IP `StateGraph` pipeline) was removed in **v0.99.149** ‚Äî since then GEODE core's runtime has been the `AgenticLoop` (`while tool_use`), not a LangGraph `StateGraph`, and `langgraph` was fully vestigial: a single `TYPE_CHECKING`-only `CompiledStateGraph` import typing a `compile_graph()` stub that only `raise`d, plus a never-non-None `_compiled_graph`, two unused properties (`thread_config` "LangGraph thread config", `checkpoint_db` "MemorySaver fallback in compile_graph"), the `build_thread_config` RunnableConfig wrapper (no production caller), a dead `enable_checkpoint` create() param, and two LangGraph warning-suppression filters. Removed all of it: the `langgraph>=1.1.0` dependency (+ `langgraph-checkpoint{,-sqlite}` deptry entries + the `langgraph.*` / `langchain_core.*` mypy overrides ‚Äî `langchain_core` was only a transitive of langgraph), `compile_graph` / `_compiled_graph` / `thread_config` / `checkpoint_db` / `build_thread_config` / `enable_checkpoint`, and the LangGraph entries in `suppress_noisy_warnings` (now a documented no-op hook). `uv.lock` drops the whole langgraph/langchain tree (ormsgpack, uuid-utils, tenacity, xxhash). `build_session_key` (the real session util) and `settings.checkpoint_db` (config field) are kept. Exposed-surface docs reframed to the AgenticLoop: README LangGraph badge removed (the `while(tool_use)-agentic loop` badge already names the engine), `CLAUDE.md` "built on LangGraph" ‚Üí AgenticLoop + the stale `graph.py` cascading-update row dropped, the `external-references` "direct foundation of GEODE's graph.py" rows removed (no `graph.py` exists). mypy 487 files clean + full gates green without langgraph, proving the dependency was dead. The LangGraph history lives in git + this entry. Codex review surfaced more dead graph-state surface, also removed: `GeodeRuntime.get_tool_state_injection` (graph-state tool injection) + the now-orphaned `set/get_async_tool_executor` contextvar in `core/tools/registry.py` (the AgenticLoop uses `ToolExecutor` directly, never that contextvar), `build_subagent_thread_config`, and the stale `LangGraph` references in `AGENTS.md`, `NOTICE`, and the committed `llms-full.txt`. `task_system.has_failed_dependency`'s "LangGraph integration" docstring was reframed. (The `site/src/data/geode/categories.ts` "pipeline" portfolio category + the orphaned `pipeline-nodes.ts` diagram still describe the removed router‚Üísynthesizer pipeline ‚Äî flagged as a portfolio-narrative decision, not mechanically rewritten.)

## [0.99.205] - 2026-06-14

### Fixed
- **Codex/ChatGPT-subscription label alignment across config + UI** (PR-CODEX-LABEL-ALIGN; operator: "GEODE ÏÑ§ÏÝïÏù¥ÎÇò UIÏóêÎèÑ CodexÏôÄ subscriptionÏùÑ ÌòºÎèôÌïú ÌëúÍ∏∞Í∞Ä ÎßéÎã§"). The `openai-codex` lane IS the ChatGPT subscription OAuth path (`core.config.credential_source.OPENAI_CODEX`), but ~18 user-facing surfaces labeled it the opaque CLI name "Codex" where they meant the ChatGPT subscription ‚Äî asymmetric with the parallel "Claude subscription" strings. Aligned to the convention **user-facing access tier/lane ‚Üí "ChatGPT (subscription)"; mechanism (CLI / OAuth backend / `~/.codex/auth.json` / `codex auth login` / the `openai-codex` provider ID / `codex-oauth` adapter) ‚Üí keep "Codex"**. Fixed: `core/llm/registry.py` (`OpenAI Codex (Plus)` ‚Üí `OpenAI (ChatGPT subscription)` ‚Äî also drops the hard-coded "Plus" tier, deferring tier display to the dynamic `chatgpt_plan_label`), `core/cli/{commands/login.py,commands/_state.py,commands/key.py,onboarding.py,effort_picker.py,doctor_bootstrap.py}`, `core/tools/definitions.json` (`/login` tool description), 3 site docs (`runtime/auth`, `run/providers`, `runtime/llm/providers` + homepage `page.tsx`), README/README.ko. Mechanism references and the `/login codex` alias are preserved. Also fixes a **latent dead branch**: `key.py`'s `if "Codex" in selected.provider:` never matched (the provider ID is lowercase `openai-codex`), so the OAuth-token-availability warning for `gpt-5.5`/`gpt-5.3-codex` picks was unreachable ‚Äî now keys on `"openai-codex"`. Builds on the gpt-5.4-mini "Codex (Plus)" PAYG mislabel guard (`test_provider_label_consistency.py`) + PR-FIX-CHATGPT-PLAN-HALLUCINATION.
